titles.en: Reward of offering Prayer in Masjidun Nabawi
Reward of offering Prayer in Masjidun Nabawi

segments: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ص صَلَاهٌ فِی مَسْجِدِی تَعْدِلُ عِنْدَ اللَّهِ عَشَرَهَ آلَافِ صَلَاهٍ فِی غَیْرِهِ مِنَ الْمَسَاجِدِ إِلَّا الْمَسْجِدَ الْحَرَامَ فَإِنَّ الصَّلَاهَ فِیهِ تَعْدِلُ مِائَهَ أَلْفِ صَلَاهٍ.
arabic.text: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ص صَلَاهٌ فِی مَسْجِدِی تَعْدِلُ عِنْدَ اللَّهِ عَشَرَهَ آلَافِ صَلَاهٍ فِی غَیْرِهِ مِنَ الْمَسَاجِدِ إِلَّا الْمَسْجِدَ الْحَرَامَ فَإِنَّ الصَّلَاهَ فِیهِ تَعْدِلُ مِائَهَ أَلْفِ صَلَاهٍ.

segments: 1.  Imam Ja’far Sadiq (a.s.) narrates from his ancestors that the Holy Prophet (sw) said, “The reward of offering a Prayer  in  Masjidun  Nabawi  is  equal  to  the  reward  of  ten thousand  Prayers  offered in  any  other  mosque  except  the Prayer offered in Masjidul Haraam whose reward is equal to the reward of a hundred thousand Prayers offered in another mosque.”
en.sayed-athar-husain-rizvi-&-sayed-maqs.text: 1.  Imam Ja’far Sadiq (a.s.) narrates from his ancestors that the Holy Prophet (sw) said, “The reward of offering a Prayer  in  Masjidun  Nabawi  is  equal  to  the  reward  of  ten thousand  Prayers  offered in  any  other  mosque  except  the Prayer offered in Masjidul Haraam whose reward is equal to the reward of a hundred thousand Prayers offered in another mosque.”

narratorChain.raw: أَبِی ره عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ جَعْفَرٍ عَنْ هَارُونَ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ مَسْعَدَهَ بْنِ صَدَقَهَ عَنِ الصَّادِقِ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ آبَائِهِ ع قَالَ
